---
title: "Presentation Guidelines"
description: "Creating Quarto RevealJS presentations with Rashid Lab branding"
---

We use [Quarto RevealJS](https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/) as
the lab's standard presentation format. This page provides the canonical
RevealJS template that mirrors the [`naimurashid.github.io`](https://naimurashid.github.io)
visual system — see [Color Palette, Typography & CSS Tokens](colors.qmd) for
the full token spec.

::: {.callout-note}
## Migration note (April 2026)

The presentation template was rewritten in April 2026 to align with the
website's three-font academic register (Archivo + Source Serif 4 + IBM Plex
Mono) and the `--rl-*` token system. Older decks using the prior
`#007FAE` link color, `Inter` / `Open Sans` body font, or `border-bottom:
3px solid` heading underlines should be updated when next opened — the
new template lives at `assets/branding/presentation-template.css`.
:::

## Why we use RevealJS

- Version-controlled slides in Markdown/Quarto
- Code execution and live outputs
- Export to PDF when needed
- Consistent branding via shared CSS template

## Required Files

Copy these into every new presentation directory:

1. **`custom.css`** — copy from `assets/branding/presentation-template.css`
2. **`RLlogoA.png`** — light-background logo, from `assets/branding/logos/`
3. **`RLlogoA-dark.png`** — dark-background logo, same path

## Quick Start (YAML frontmatter)

```yaml
---
title: "Your Presentation Title"
subtitle: "Optional subtitle"
author: "Naim Rashid, PhD"
institute: "UNC Gillings School of Global Public Health"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.css]
    logo: RLlogoA.png
    footer: "Rashid Lab · UNC Chapel Hill"
    slide-number: c/t
    transition: fade
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    incremental: false
    fig-align: center
    code-overflow: wrap
    css-includes:
      - https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Archivo:wght@400;500;600;700&family=Source+Serif+4:ital,wght@0,400;0,600;0,700;1,400&family=IBM+Plex+Mono:wght@400;500&display=swap
---
```

## CSS Template

The full canonical template is in
`assets/branding/presentation-template.css`. It uses the same `--rl-*`
tokens as the website. Below is a minimal viable copy you can paste
directly into a project's `custom.css`:

```scss
/*-- scss:defaults --*/

@import url("https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Archivo:wght@400;500;600;700&family=Source+Serif+4:ital,wght@0,400;0,600;0,700;1,400&family=IBM+Plex+Mono:wght@400;500&display=swap");

// Brand palette
$rl-navy:          #13294B;
$rl-carolina:      #4B9CD3;
$rl-carolina-deep: #2E6A9F;
$rl-graphite:      #5B6670;
$rl-ink:           #1A202C;
$rl-fog:           #F4F6F8;
$rl-mist:          #E6EAEF;

// RevealJS variable mapping
$body-bg:                    #FFFFFF;
$body-color:                 $rl-ink;
$link-color:                 $rl-carolina-deep;
$presentation-heading-color: $rl-navy;
$presentation-heading-font:  "Archivo", sans-serif;
$presentation-font:          "Archivo", sans-serif;

/*-- scss:rules --*/

// Make tokens available at runtime
:root {
  --rl-navy:          #13294B;
  --rl-carolina:      #4B9CD3;
  --rl-carolina-deep: #2E6A9F;
  --rl-graphite:      #5B6670;
  --rl-ink:           #1A202C;
  --rl-fog:           #F4F6F8;
  --rl-mist:          #E6EAEF;
  --rl-font-sans:  "Archivo", sans-serif;
  --rl-font-serif: "Source Serif 4", Georgia, serif;
  --rl-font-mono:  "IBM Plex Mono", Menlo, monospace;
}

// Title slide — serif title (book-cover register), sans subtitle
#title-slide {
  text-align: left;
  padding-left: 5%;

  .title {
    color: var(--rl-navy);
    font-family: var(--rl-font-serif);
    font-size: 2.4em;
    font-weight: 600;
    letter-spacing: -0.01em;
    line-height: 1.1;
    margin-bottom: 0.5em;
  }
  .subtitle {
    color: var(--rl-graphite);
    font-family: var(--rl-font-sans);
    font-size: 1.3em;
    font-weight: 400;
    margin-bottom: 1.25em;
  }
  .author {
    color: var(--rl-ink);
    font-size: 1.0em;
    font-weight: 500;
  }
  .institute, .date {
    color: var(--rl-graphite);
    font-family: var(--rl-font-mono);
    font-size: 0.75em;
    letter-spacing: 0.04em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
  }
}

// Headings — sans (deck legibility) over serif (web body register)
.reveal h1, .reveal h2, .reveal h3 {
  color: var(--rl-navy);
  font-family: var(--rl-font-sans);
  font-weight: 600;
  letter-spacing: -0.01em;
  // No decorative bottom-border. Use whitespace + size hierarchy instead.
}
.reveal h1 { font-size: 1.8em; }
.reveal h2 { font-size: 1.4em; }
.reveal h3 { font-size: 1.1em; }

// Body text & links
.reveal { font-family: var(--rl-font-sans); }
.reveal p, .reveal li { color: var(--rl-ink); line-height: 1.45; }
.reveal a { color: var(--rl-carolina-deep); text-decoration: none; }
.reveal a:hover { color: var(--rl-navy); text-decoration: underline; }

// Code — mono, fog background, no decorative left-stripe
.reveal pre, .reveal code {
  font-family: var(--rl-font-mono);
}
.reveal pre {
  background: var(--rl-fog);
  border: 1px solid var(--rl-mist);
  border-radius: 4px;
  padding: 0.6em 0.8em;
}
.reveal code {
  background: var(--rl-fog);
  color: var(--rl-ink);
  padding: 0.1em 0.3em;
  border-radius: 3px;
}

// Tables — hairline borders only, no navy header bar
.reveal table {
  border-collapse: collapse;
  font-size: 0.8em;
  margin: 1em auto;
}
.reveal table th {
  border-bottom: 2px solid var(--rl-navy);
  color: var(--rl-navy);
  font-family: var(--rl-font-mono);
  font-size: 0.85em;
  font-weight: 500;
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
  padding: 0.4em 0.8em;
  text-align: left;
  text-transform: uppercase;
}
.reveal table td {
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--rl-mist);
  padding: 0.4em 0.8em;
}

// Slide chrome — slide number + footer in mono caps
.reveal .slide-number {
  color: var(--rl-graphite);
  font-family: var(--rl-font-mono);
  font-size: 0.7em;
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
}
.reveal .footer {
  color: var(--rl-graphite);
  font-family: var(--rl-font-mono);
  font-size: 0.65em;
  letter-spacing: 0.06em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
}

// Callouts — hairline left border in token color
.callout-note      { border-left: 3px solid var(--rl-carolina-deep); }
.callout-tip       { border-left: 3px solid #2E8B6D; }
.callout-warning   { border-left: 3px solid #C48A1A; }
.callout-important { border-left: 3px solid #C75050; }
```

## Slide Structure

### Title Slide

```markdown
---
title: "Constrained Bayesian Optimization for Clinical Trials"
subtitle: "ARPA-H ADAPT Platform Trial"
author: "Naim Rashid, PhD"
institute: "UNC Gillings School of Global Public Health"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.css]
---
```

### Content Slides

```markdown
## Background {.smaller}

- Point one with **emphasis**
- Point two with `code`
- Point three with [link](https://example.com)

::: {.notes}
Speaker notes go here
:::
```

### Two-Column Layout

```markdown
## Comparison

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Method A**

- Feature 1
- Feature 2
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Method B**

- Feature 1
- Feature 2
:::

::::
```

### Code with Output

````
## R Example

```{{r}}
#| echo: true
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +
  geom_point(color = "#2E6A9F") +
  theme_minimal(base_family = "Archivo")
```
````

## PDF Export

```bash
quarto render presentation.qmd --to pdf
```

Or use the browser's print function (Ctrl/Cmd + P) when viewing the HTML slides.

## Tips

1. **Keep slides minimal** — one idea per slide
2. **Use high-resolution figures** — export at 300 DPI minimum
3. **Test on projector** — colors may appear differently
4. **Sans for slide headings, serif on title slide only** — serif is harder to read at projector distance for body content
5. **Mono for metadata** — slide numbers, footer, dates, table column headers
6. **Version control** — commit slides to GitHub
7. **Include speaker notes** — use `::: {.notes}` blocks

## F31 Lab Meeting Presentation Template

For your first research presentation to the lab, use the **F31-structured presentation template**. This template organizes your talk around the sections of an NIH F31 (Ruth L. Kirschstein Predoctoral Individual NRSA) fellowship application, helping you think through your research narrative early in your PhD.

**Download:** [f31-lab-meeting-template.qmd](../assets/branding/f31-lab-meeting-template.qmd.txt) (right-click → Save As, rename to `.qmd`)

The template includes:

- **12 slides** mirroring F31 sections (Specific Aims, Significance, Innovation, Approach, Training Goals, etc.)
- **Speaker notes** with F31 guidance, page limits, and tips for each section
- **Direct NIH links** to the current FORMS-I application instructions
- **Page limit reference table** for all F31 sections
- Lab branding (uses the same `custom.css` and logo as all presentations)

### How to Use

1. Copy the template and branding files into your project directory:
   ```bash
   cp ~/rashid-lab-setup/lab-handbook/assets/branding/f31-lab-meeting-template.qmd.txt ./my-presentation.qmd
   cp ~/rashid-lab-setup/lab-handbook/assets/branding/presentation-template.css ./custom.css
   cp ~/rashid-lab-setup/lab-handbook/assets/branding/logos/RLlogoA.png ./assets/
   ```
2. Rename and edit the `.qmd` file with your content
3. Press `S` during presentation to see the F31 coaching notes
4. Preview with `quarto preview your-presentation.qmd`

::: {.callout-note}
Updated for **FORMS-I** (effective January 25, 2025). Section names reflect the revised fellowship application structure. See the [NIH changes summary](https://grants.nih.gov/policy-and-compliance/policy-topics/peer-review/revisions-nih-fellowship-application-review-process/changes-to-fellowship-applications) for details.
:::

## Creating Presentations with Claude Code

Claude Code can help create and edit Quarto presentations:

```
> Create a new Quarto RevealJS presentation with lab branding
> Add a slide showing the simulation results figure
> Help me format this code block for the methods slide
```

Claude knows the lab's color scheme and styling conventions.

See [Claude Code Guide](../claude-code/index.qmd) for more on using Claude with Quarto.
